concept.conceptName: aPTT actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay

en.name: aPTT ratio in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The aPTT ratio is a transformation of the activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) expressed as the patient's time divided by the laboratory's normal control time. It is one of the standard outputs of a coagulation analyser, alongside the aPTT in seconds. A healthy patient has an aPTT ratio of ~1. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a coagulation analyser on platelet poor plasma (PPP). Results are reported as a dimensionless ratio [1]. **Note: aPTT ratio vs aPTT.** The aPTT ratio (this concept set) and the aPTT in seconds are two different representations of the same underlying physical measurement, with different normalisations: - **aPTT ratio** — patient time divided by the laboratory's normal control time (this concept set). - **aPTT (seconds)** — raw measurement; depends on the laboratory's reagent. Captured by a separate concept set, "aPTT in blood". The two representations are **not interconvertible** without the laboratory's local normal control. The data dictionary keeps them addressable independently — analyses needing a homogeneous variable should pick one representation rather than merging across. ## Included Concepts The set contains one LOINC "Lab Test" concept: - *40766287 / 63561-5 — aPTT actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay*: dimensionless ratio (patient/normal control). **Note on units.** The aPTT ratio is dimensionless. LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS` lists no unit; some source systems report no unit, others report `1` or `{ratio}`. All represent the same dimensionless quantity. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The aPTT-derived ratios related to protein C / APC resistance assays (e.g. `48590-4`, `52766-3`, `52767-1`) are deliberately not part of this set — they are specialised tests with a different clinical question. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports "aPTT ratio" (patient/control, dimensionless): `40766287 / 63561-5 — aPTT actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay`. - **Source reports aPTT in seconds**: do **not** map to this concept set. The aPTT in seconds has its own concept set, "aPTT in blood". - **Do not confuse with**: aPTT in seconds, prothrombin ratio, INR, activated clotting time (ACT), APC resistance test, thrombin time, or anti-Xa heparin level. ## References [1] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/ [2] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 63561-5 — aPTT actual/normal in Platelet poor plasma by Coagulation assay. Available: https://loinc.org/63561-5/
